canonical_definition: A large language model used as an automated evaluator to assess the safety and alignment of other models' generated responses.
papers: Beyond Interpretability: The Gains of Feature Monosemanticity on Model Robustness
Breach By A Thousand Leaks: Unsafe Information Leakage in 'Safe' AI Responses

definitions.text: A large language model used as an automated evaluator to assess the safety and alignment of other models' generated responses.
definitions.paper: Beyond Interpretability: The Gains of Feature Monosemanticity on Model Robustness

definitions.text: A model used as a safety filter or judge to curate a dataset by predicting the probability that questions are dangerous, and also used as an input/output filter in experiments.
definitions.paper: Breach By A Thousand Leaks: Unsafe Information Leakage in 'Safe' AI Responses
